query: What is the structure of the chart of accounts in Odoo 18?
ground_truth: Odoo 18 organizes the chart of accounts (CoA) into a hierarchical structure with account types that determine financial reporting behavior. The top-level classifications are Assets, Liabilities, Equity, Income, and Expenses. Each account record (account.account) has a code (e.g., 1100 for Cash, 2100 for Accounts Payable, 5100 for Operating Expenses), a name, an account_type field (e.g., asset_receivable, asset_cash, liability_payable, liability_current, expense, income, equity), and optional group and tag assignments. The CoA can be loaded from a localization package (e.g., l10n_ph for Philippines, which includes Philippine-standard account codes and tax templates) or configured manually via Accounting > Configuration > Chart of Accounts. Account groups (account.group) allow sub-totaling on the balance sheet and P&L — groups are defined by code prefix ranges. Tags (account.account.tag) enable additional classification for tax reporting and analytics. Accounts can be marked as reconcilable (allow_reconciliation=True) for AR/AP matching. The internal_group field maps account types to balance sheet sections. Navigation: Accounting > Configuration > Chart of Accounts. [Source: Odoo 18 Documentation § Chart of Accounts]
expected_kb_topic: Chart of accounts structure, account types, account groups, localization packages

query: Which OCA module handles bank reconciliation in Odoo 18?
ground_truth: The OCA module account_reconcile_oca (from the OCA/account-reconcile repository) provides enhanced bank reconciliation for Odoo 18 CE. It replaces the Enterprise-only reconciliation widget with an open-source alternative that supports manual and rule-based matching of bank statement lines to journal items. The module adds a dedicated reconciliation interface accessible from Accounting > Bank > Reconcile. Complementary modules include: account_statement_import_online for automated bank feeds, account_reconcile_model_oca for configurable matching rules, and the account_statement_import family (account_statement_import, account_statement_import_ofx, account_statement_import_camt, account_statement_import_txt_xlsx) from OCA/bank-statement-import for importing bank statements in OFX, CAMT.053, CSV, and XLSX formats. The reconciliation process matches bank statement lines (account.bank.statement.line) against journal items (account.move.line) using automatic rules or manual selection. Reconciliation models (account.reconcile.model) define matching criteria like partner name, reference, or amount. The module is widely adopted across the OCA community with active maintenance and CI passing on the 18.0 branch. [Source: OCA account-reconcile repository § account_reconcile_oca, OCA bank-statement-import § Module Inventory]
expected_kb_topic: Bank reconciliation OCA modules, account_reconcile_oca, statement import

query: How do I configure BIR withholding taxes in Odoo?
ground_truth: BIR withholding taxes in Odoo are configured through tax records (account.tax) via Accounting > Configuration > Taxes. For Expanded Withholding Tax (EWT), create tax entries for each BIR rate: 1% (WE010 — contractors/subcontractors), 2% (WE020 — professional fees, services), 5% (WE050 — rentals), 10% (WE100 — professional services to individuals), 15% (WE150 — certain income payments). Each tax record must be configured with: Tax Type set to 'Purchase', Tax Computation set to 'Percentage of Price', Amount set to the negative rate (e.g., -2.00 for WE020 — negative because it reduces the payable), the correct BIR Alphanumeric Tax Code (ATC) in the description or label field, a mapping to the withholding tax payable account (e.g., 2151 - Withholding Tax Payable), and assignment to a 'Withholding Taxes' tax group. The l10n_ph localization provides baseline tax templates for Philippine operations, but BIR-specific EWT rates require manual configuration or the ipai_finance_ppm module. Taxes can be assigned as defaults on vendor partner records (Accounting tab > Fiscal Position or purchase tax fields). When applied to a vendor bill, the EWT tax reduces the amount payable — the journal entry debits the expense account, credits AP (reduced), and credits WH Tax Payable. The tax report (Accounting > Reporting > Tax Report) shows all withholding amounts for quarterly filing. [Source: BIR Compliance Knowledge Base § Withholding Tax Configuration, Tax Guru PH § EWT Rate Schedule]
expected_kb_topic: BIR withholding tax setup, EWT rates, tax code mapping, l10n_ph

query: What is the recommended PostgreSQL backup strategy for an Odoo database?
ground_truth: The recommended PostgreSQL backup strategy for Odoo follows a 3-2-1 approach: 3 copies, 2 different media, 1 offsite. Daily pg_dump with --format=custom produces compressed backups of the Odoo database (odoo_dev for development, odoo_staging for staging, odoo for production). WAL archiving via pg_basebackup or continuous archiving enables point-in-time recovery (PITR). Backup schedule: full daily dump at 02:00 UTC, continuous WAL archiving, weekly validation restore to a test database. Retention: 30 days for daily dumps, 7 days for WAL segments. For Azure-managed PostgreSQL (Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server v16), Azure Backup provides automated snapshots with geo-redundant storage — this handles both database and configuration backup with configurable retention. Critical: always backup the Odoo filestore (/var/lib/odoo/filestore or Azure Blob Storage for cloud deployments) alongside the database, as the filestore contains attachments, images, and binary data referenced by database records. Backup scripts can be scheduled via cron jobs or Azure Automation. Restore testing should verify both database integrity and filestore consistency. For large databases, consider pg_dump --jobs for parallel dump. [Source: PostgreSQL Admin Knowledge Base § Backup Strategy, Local Platform SSOT § Backup and Recovery]
expected_kb_topic: PostgreSQL backup, pg_dump, WAL archiving, PITR, filestore backup

query: What are the module naming conventions for custom modules?
ground_truth: Custom modules follow the naming convention ipai_<domain>_<feature> where ipai is the organization prefix, <domain> identifies the functional area (e.g., finance, auth, ai, hr, inventory, sales, platform), and <feature> describes the specific capability. Examples: ipai_finance_ppm (finance portfolio performance management), ipai_auth_oidc (authentication via OpenID Connect), ipai_ai_copilot (AI assistant integration), ipai_ai_tools (AI utility functions). The module philosophy is Config > OCA > Delta: (1) first try built-in Odoo 18 configuration options, (2) then adopt vetted OCA community modules for common patterns, (3) only create ipai_* modules for truly custom needs that neither Config nor OCA satisfies. Module directories live under addons/ipai/ and must include: __manifest__.py with proper depends list, version following 18.0.x.y.z scheme, license set to LGPL-3, author, and category. Each module must have __init__.py with model imports but never import tests from the root __init__.py (Odoo discovers tests via tests/__init__.py automatically). Additional required files: README.rst following OCA template, tests/ directory with unit tests, static/description/icon.png. All modules must pass pre-commit hooks and repo_health.sh checks before merge. Deprecated modules are listed in CLAUDE.md with replacement and deprecation date. [Source: Repository Module Doctrine § Naming Conventions, § Module Philosophy]
expected_kb_topic: Module naming ipai_* pattern, Config > OCA > Delta philosophy, manifest requirements

query: How do I set up multi-company operations in Odoo 18?
ground_truth: Odoo 18 supports multi-company operations natively through the res.company model. Setup involves: (1) Create additional companies under Settings > Companies, each with its own chart of accounts (CoA), currency, fiscal localization (l10n_xx), and tax configuration. (2) Configure inter-company rules under Settings > Companies > Inter-Company Transactions to auto-generate counter-documents — e.g., a Sales Order in Company A automatically creates a Purchase Order in Company B, and vice versa. (3) Assign users to companies via the Allowed Companies field on res.users, with one company set as the default company. Users can only access records belonging to companies in their allowed list. (4) Security rules (ir.rule) enforce record-level company isolation using the company_ids domain filter — this is how Odoo enforces data separation between companies at the ORM level. Users switch active companies via the company switcher in the top navigation bar. Each company maintains separate fiscal years, journals, bank accounts, and tax configurations. Shared data includes: product catalogs (with per-company pricing via pricelists), contact lists (res.partner), and user accounts. For accounting, each company has its own journal entries, reports, and closing process. Inter-company journal entries require explicit configuration. Cross-company access requires the user to have both companies in their allowed list — never suggest admin override or company switching as a workaround for access issues. [Source: Odoo 18 Documentation § Multi-Company]
expected_kb_topic: Multi-company setup, inter-company transactions, company security rules, company switcher

query: What OCA modules handle expense management?
ground_truth: The OCA ecosystem provides several modules for expense management in Odoo 18 CE, extending the built-in hr_expense module. The primary modules from the OCA/hr-expense repository include: hr_expense_sequence (auto-numbering expense reports with configurable sequences), hr_expense_advance_clearing (advance payment workflow — request advance, submit clearing with receipts, reconcile the difference), hr_expense_payment (link approved expense reports to OCA payment orders for batch processing), and hr_expense_cancel (allow cancellation of posted expense entries with proper audit trail). From the OCA/account-financial-tools repository: account_move_budget tracks budgets that expense reports draw against. These modules integrate with OCA payment order modules (account_payment_order from OCA/bank-payment) for batch vendor payments. The built-in hr_expense module provides the core workflow: employee submits expenses > manager approves > accountant posts journal entries > payment registered. OCA modules add multi-level validation, advance/clearing, budget checking, and payment order integration on top of this base. Module quality: all OCA modules pass CI on the 18.0 branch and are maintained by active OCA contributors. [Source: OCA HR-Expense Repository § Module Inventory, OCA account-financial-tools § Budget]
expected_kb_topic: OCA expense modules, hr_expense_sequence, advance clearing, payment integration

query: What are the BIR 2307 form requirements for withholding tax certificates?
ground_truth: BIR Form 2307 (Certificate of Creditable Tax Withheld at Source) is issued by the withholding agent (payor) to the payee for each transaction or period where EWT is withheld. Required fields include: TIN of both payor and payee, registered name and address of both parties, ATC (Alphanumeric Tax Code) identifying the income payment type (e.g., WE020 for professional fees, WE100 for professional services to individuals), period covered (monthly or quarterly — Q1: Jan-Mar, Q2: Apr-Jun, Q3: Jul-Sep, Q4: Oct-Dec), gross income amount (the base amount before tax), tax rate applied (e.g., 2%, 5%, 10%), and tax amount withheld. The form must be issued on or before the 20th day of the month following the close of the taxable quarter. Three copies are produced: original for payee (to use as creditable tax certificate when filing income tax returns), duplicate for BIR, and triplicate retained by payor for records. The payee uses received 2307 forms to claim tax credits in their income tax return (ITR). In Odoo, the source data comes from posted vendor bills (account.move with move_type='in_invoice') that have withholding tax lines — the system extracts payor/payee TIN, ATC from tax records, base amounts, and computed withholding. Supporting regulation: BIR Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 as amended. [Source: BIR Compliance Knowledge Base § Form 2307 Requirements, Tax Guru PH § Withholding Certificate Processing]
expected_kb_topic: BIR 2307 form fields, ATC codes, filing deadlines, creditable withholding certificate

query: What is the platform deployment architecture?
ground_truth: The platform runs on Azure Container Apps (ACA) behind Azure Front Door for global edge routing, TLS termination, and WAF protection. The insightpulseai.com domain is managed via Azure DNS (authoritative, delegated from Squarespace). The architecture consists of: Odoo 18 CE application container running on ACA with horizontal auto-scaling (1-5 replicas based on HTTP concurrency), Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (v16, General Purpose tier) as the managed database, Azure Blob Storage for the Odoo filestore (attachments, images), and Azure Key Vault for secrets management (database credentials, SMTP passwords, API keys). CI runs on GitHub Actions; deployment promotion flows through Azure DevOps pipelines with environment gates: dev > staging > production. Three environments: dev (database: odoo_dev), staging (database: odoo_staging), and production (database: odoo) — each with dedicated ACA environments and separate database instances. Monitoring uses Azure Monitor for infrastructure metrics and Application Insights for application-level telemetry. Alert rules are configured for container restarts, CPU usage, HTTP 5xx errors, and zero-replica conditions. Mail is handled via Zoho SMTP (smtp.zoho.com:587). The AI/agent plane runs on Azure AI Foundry (ipai-copilot-resource, East US 2) as a separate service plane from the Odoo operational system of record. [Source: Local Platform SSOT § Deployment Architecture, § Security Model]
expected_kb_topic: Azure Container Apps, Front Door, PostgreSQL Flexible Server, deployment pipeline

query: How does the journal entry workflow work in Odoo 18?
ground_truth: Journal entries in Odoo 18 follow a state machine workflow on the account.move model: Draft > Posted > Cancelled. In Draft state, the entry is editable — users can modify lines, amounts, accounts, and dates. Posting (action_post method) validates that debits equal credits across all lines, assigns a sequence number from the journal's sequence (e.g., INV/2026/0001 for sales, BILL/2026/0001 for purchases, MISC/2026/0001 for miscellaneous), and locks the entry from further edits. The posted entry then appears in all financial reports: general ledger, trial balance, balance sheet, P&L, and aged partner balances. Cancellation (button_cancel) reverses the posting and returns to draft, but only if the journal allows cancellation and the lock date hasn't passed — lock dates (Settings > Accounting > Lock Dates) prevent modifications to entries before a cutoff date, ensuring period integrity. For reversals, Odoo creates a new reversal entry (action_reverse method) rather than deleting the original, maintaining a full audit trail — the reversal entry has all amounts negated and references the original entry. Access to post entries is controlled by the group_account_manager security group; draft creation is available to group_account_invoice. Navigation: Accounting > Accounting > Journal Entries (for miscellaneous), or Customers > Invoices / Vendors > Bills for specific types. [Source: Odoo 18 Documentation § Journal Entries, § Lock Dates]
expected_kb_topic: Journal entry lifecycle, account.move states, posting, reversal, audit trail
